content.problemStatement:
In computing, the collection of **four bits** is called a **nibble**.

Chef defines a program as:


**Good**, if it takes exactly XXX nibbles of memory, where XXX is a positive integer.
**Not Good**, otherwise.
Given a program which takes NNN **bits** of memory, determine whether it is **Good** or **Not Good**.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of bits taken by the program.


Output Format
For each test case, output Good\texttt{Good}Good or Not Good\texttt{Not Good}Not Good in a single line.
You may print each character of Good\texttt{Good}Good or Not Good\texttt{Not Good}Not Good in uppercase or lowercase (for example, GoOd\texttt{GoOd}GoOd, GOOD\texttt{GOOD}GOOD, good\texttt{good}good will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N – the memory required by the program in bits.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "Good" if N is exactly divisible by 4 (i.e., the program occupies an integer number of nibbles) otherwise output "Not Good". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 8 bits = 2 nibbles (integer) → Good. 17 bits = 4.25 nibbles → Not Good. 52 bits = 13 nibbles → Good. 3 bits = 0.75 nibbles → Not Good.
4 bits = 1 nibble → Good. 5 bits is not divisible by 4 → Not Good. 1000 bits = 250 nibbles → Good.
Only 4 is divisible by 4 among the given numbers.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to check whether N mod 4 equals 0. If it does, the number of bits corresponds to an integer number of nibbles (since 1 nibble = 4 bits) and the program is Good; otherwise it is Not Good. This check is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used irrespective of input size.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){ long long N; scanf("%lld",&N); if(N%4==0) printf("Good\n"); else printf("Not Good\n"); } return 0; }
